prompt:
A surreal dreamlike landscape painting showing five distinct terraced garden levels cascading downward from the upper left corner to the lower right corner. Each garden terrace is a lush, vibrant ecosystem separated from the others by steep rocky cliffs and bands of mist.

Within each individual terrace, flowers, trees, and vegetation grow progressively taller, more colorful, and more luminous from left to right — every garden clearly ascends and flourishes within its own boundaries. The leftmost edge of each terrace has sparse seedlings; the rightmost edge has towering blooms and rich canopy.

But each successive terrace is positioned dramatically lower than the one above. The topmost garden sits in brilliant golden sunlight on a high plateau bathed in warm light. The second terrace is lower but still bright. The third is in cool shadow. The fourth is dim. The bottommost garden, despite its own internal flourishing, sits in deep blue twilight near dark still water.

The overall composition descends unmistakably from radiant light to approaching darkness, from great height to low depth, even as every individual garden ascends from sparse to abundant.

Ethereal atmosphere, volumetric god rays, rich saturated colors, painterly quality, wide panoramic composition, dreamlike surreal quality. No text, no numbers, no words, no symbols, no labels, no charts, no graphs, no mathematical notation, no arrows, no diagrams, no human figures.
visual_logic: Terraced gardens each bloom upward left-to-right, but terraces cascade sharply downward overall. Every subgroup ascends; the combined landscape descends. The local-global reversal is simultaneously visible at both scales.
